signals.title: Building effective agents
signals.summary: A public engineering article frames agents as systems that combine model calls, tools, workflow control and feedback loops.
requirements: Choose simple workflow patterns before adding open-ended agent autonomy.
Make tool boundaries and failure handling explicit in the system design.
signals.currentGap: Need a public workflow example that shows when a fixed workflow is enough and when an agent loop is justified.
signals.nextAction: Use the first Agent Signals page to track workflow patterns and link them to future experiments.

signals.title: MCP turns tool access into a stable agent interface
signals.summary: MCP documentation makes a strong case for standardizing how agents discover tools, data sources and execution context.
requirements: Understand how agent tools are described, authorized and invoked across clients.
Design integrations with a contract that can survive tool and client changes.
signals.currentGap: Need a small documented MCP-style integration example or comparison against current CLI automation.
signals.nextAction: Add MCP to the experience map after the signals module is live.

signals.title: Coding agents need repeatable evaluation, not only demos
signals.summary: Recent coding-agent practice suggests that visible productivity gains need a repeatable eval loop to stay trustworthy.
requirements: Define small task suites that can be rerun after model, prompt or tool changes.
Record failure modes in a structured format that can feed later improvements.
signals.currentGap: Token telemetry is live, but there is not yet a public eval record connected to the dashboard.
signals.nextAction: Design a minimal eval JSON contract after Agent Signals reaches MVP.

signals.title: Agent platforms are converging on tools, tracing and deployable workflows
signals.summary: Public product updates around agent building emphasize hosted tools, orchestration, tracing and deployment paths.
requirements: Treat tracing and inspection as core agent engineering features.
Separate prototype prompts from deployable workflow contracts.
signals.currentGap: The dashboard shows token telemetry, but not yet workflow traces or deployment notes.
signals.nextAction: Keep observability as a recurring capability tag and connect it to future project notes.

signals.title: Multi-agent frameworks need clear role boundaries
signals.summary: Open-source multi-agent frameworks show how quickly coordination, role design and observability become system concerns.
requirements: Model agent roles and handoffs explicitly instead of relying on implicit chat flow.
Log enough state to debug coordination failures.
signals.currentGap: Need one public comparison between a single-agent workflow and a multi-agent workflow.
signals.nextAction: Track multi-agent examples as signals before deciding whether to build a demo.

signals.title: Agent engineer roles ask for productized automation and evaluation
signals.summary: Public role descriptions repeatedly connect LLM agents with tool integration, workflow automation, evaluation and deployment ownership.
requirements: Show deployed systems rather than isolated prompts.
Explain data flow, privacy boundaries and operational failure modes.
signals.currentGap: The public dashboard has deployment and token telemetry, but needs a visible learning-signal loop.
signals.nextAction: Ship the Agent Signals module as the next proof point.
